channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Fully Funded UN Climate Change Youth Event 2026: Apply Now for the ACE Hub Youth Event (Global Opportunity for Young Climate Leaders)
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
If you are a young climate advocate, environmental innovator, or aspiring global change-maker, the 2026 Action for Climate Empowerment (ACE) Hub Youth Event offers one of the most impactful international platforms to elevate your voice, build your skills, and collaborate with global peers. Organized under the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC), this […]

The post Fully Funded UN Climate Change Youth Event 2026: Apply Now for the ACE Hub Youth Event (Global Opportunity for Young Climate Leaders) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are a young climate advocate, environmental innovator, or aspiring global change-maker, the 2026 Action for Climate Empowerment (ACE) Hub Youth Event offers one of the most impactful international platforms to elevate your voice, build your skills, and collaborate with global peers. Organized under the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC), this prestigious event is designed to empower youth to lead meaningful climate action at all levels.

What Is the ACE Hub Youth Event?

The ACE Hub Youth Event is a flagship international gathering under the broader Action for Climate Empowerment (ACE) Hub, a global initiative focused on equipping individuals—especially youth—with the knowledge, skills, and tools to actively participate in climate action.

The ACE Hub itself is a collaboration between UN Climate Change and the Government of North Rhine-Westphalia in Germany, supporting the implementation of climate education, public awareness, training, and participation under the Paris Agreement.

This annual youth event is specifically designed to:

  • Build the capacity of young leaders to drive climate solutions
  • Promote collaboration and innovation in climate initiatives
  • Facilitate global networking and knowledge exchange
  • Strengthen youth engagement in policy and grassroots action

Why You Should Apply

Participating in the ACE Hub Youth Event is not just about attending a conference—it is about becoming part of a global movement shaping climate policy and action.

Key Benefits

  • Gain hands-on training in climate advocacy and project design
  • Engage with international experts and policymakers
  • Collaborate with youth leaders from across the world
  • Strengthen your leadership and communication skills
  • Expand your global network in climate and sustainability
  • Opportunity to attend sessions linked to UN climate negotiations

Participants also benefit from immersive learning experiences including workshops, peer-led sessions, and expert-led discussions.

Who Can Apply? (Eligibility Criteria)

This opportunity targets young climate leaders and changemakers who are actively engaged in climate-related initiatives.

Eligible Category of People

  • Youth leaders
  • Climate activists and advocates
  • Environmental professionals
  • Students and early-career researchers
  • Community organizers working on climate solutions

General Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Be aged 18 to 35 years
  • Have proficiency in English (spoken and written)
  • Be able to attend the full event in person in Bonn, Germany
  • Demonstrate interest or experience in climate action
  • Be engaged in areas such as:
    • Climate education
    • Public awareness
    • Community-based climate initiatives
    • Policy advocacy

Geographic Eligibility

  • Open to all countries worldwide
  • Additional slots available for local youth from North Rhine-Westphalia (Germany)

Program Structure and Activities

The ACE Hub Youth Event is structured as a 3-day intensive experience combining learning, collaboration, and innovation.

What to Expect

  • Day 1: Skills-building workshops and peer-led sessions
  • Day 2: Collaborative group work on climate solutions
  • Day 3: Networking, presentations, and knowledge exchange

Participants will:

  • Present their own climate initiatives
  • Work on solving real-world environmental challenges
  • Participate in expert-led sessions on communication and advocacy

The program emphasizes interactive learning and practical application, ensuring participants leave with actionable skills.

Themes and Focus Areas

The 2026 edition continues to focus on youth leadership and climate entrepreneurship, with strong emphasis on:

  • Community-based climate action
  • Innovation and sustainable solutions
  • Climate communication and advocacy
  • Local-to-global climate impact strategies

Funding and Financial Support

While the event includes both funded and self-funded participation options:

  • A limited number of international participants may receive funding support
  • Additional participants may attend through self-funding or independent accreditation

Funding (when available) may cover:

  • Travel expenses
  • Accommodation
  • Participation costs

Why This Opportunity Matters

Climate change is one of the most urgent global challenges of our time, and youth are at the forefront of driving solutions. The ACE Hub Youth Event directly contributes to empowering young people under Article 6 of the UNFCCC and Article 12 of the Paris Agreement, which focus on education, awareness, and participation.

By participating, you are not only gaining skills—you are becoming part of a global ecosystem working toward sustainable and equitable climate solutions.

How to Apply

To apply for the ACE Hub Youth Event 2026:

  1. Complete the official online application form
  2. Provide details of your climate-related experience
  3. Demonstrate your motivation and impact potential
  4. Submit before the deadline

Apply here

Deadline

10th April 2026

The ACE Hub Youth Event 2026 in Bonn, Germany is more than just a conference—it is a transformative experience for young individuals committed to climate action. Whether you are already leading initiatives or just starting your journey, this opportunity provides the platform, resources, and network needed to amplify your impact.

For more information click here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Fully Funded UN Climate Change Youth Event 2026: Apply Now for the ACE Hub Youth Event (Global Opportunity for Young Climate Leaders) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Anzisha Coach Job Opportunity 2026 (Remote) at African Leadership Academy: Empower Africa’s Next Generation of Entrepreneurs
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
If you are passionate about entrepreneurship, youth development, and creating meaningful impact across Africa, the Anzisha Coach position at African Leadership Academy offers a unique and transformative career path. This role sits at the intersection of education and entrepreneurship, two of the most critical drivers of economic growth and innovation across the African continent. Through […]

The post Anzisha Coach Job Opportunity 2026 (Remote) at African Leadership Academy: Empower Africa’s Next Generation of Entrepreneurs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are passionate about entrepreneurship, youth development, and creating meaningful impact across Africa, the Anzisha Coach position at African Leadership Academy offers a unique and transformative career path.

This role sits at the intersection of education and entrepreneurship, two of the most critical drivers of economic growth and innovation across the African continent. Through its flagship initiative, Anzisha Program, the Academy is shaping a generation of young entrepreneurs who are building sustainable businesses and creating jobs in their communities.

The position is ideal for experienced professionals who want to mentor, coach, and directly influence the growth of early-stage entrepreneurs across Africa.

About the Anzisha Program

The Anzisha Program is a partnership-driven initiative supported by organizations such as Mastercard Foundation. It focuses on empowering very young entrepreneurs (typically under 22 years old) to build and scale impactful businesses.

The program consists of three key stages:

1. Idea Program (3 Months)

A foundational stage where young entrepreneurs:

  • Develop a Minimum Viable Product (MVP)
  • Validate business ideas with real-world sales or impact
  • Prepare for scaling opportunities

2. Build Program (5 Months)

Focused on:

  • Strengthening business models
  • Driving measurable growth (revenue, jobs, impact)
  • Building sustainable and scalable ventures

3. Venture Building Fellowship

A multi-year fellowship that:

  • Provides continuous business and leadership support
  • Recognizes outstanding young entrepreneurs
  • Helps scale ventures across markets

Role Overview: What Does an Anzisha Coach Do?

As an Anzisha Coach, you will play a pivotal role in shaping Africa’s entrepreneurial ecosystem by working directly with young founders.

This is not just a coaching role—it is a high-impact leadership position involving mentorship, program design, ecosystem building, and strategic execution.

You will:

  • Provide one-on-one coaching
  • Support entrepreneurs in business development
  • Facilitate training sessions and workshops
  • Connect founders to networks, partners, and investors
  • Track progress using data-driven insights

Key Responsibilities Explained in Detail

1. Coaching and Mentorship

You will serve as a trusted advisor to young entrepreneurs by:

  • Guiding business strategy, financial planning, and marketing execution
  • Helping founders navigate real-world challenges
  • Building strong, empathy-driven relationships
  • Supporting participants across multiple programs, including AL for Education initiatives

You will also travel across African countries to:

  • Visit entrepreneurs
  • Understand local business environments
  • Provide in-person support where necessary

2. Program Development and Project Management

This role requires strong operational leadership. You will:

  • Design and implement entrepreneurship programs
  • Lead workshops on leadership and business growth
  • Develop innovative support strategies for young founders
  • Manage projects end-to-end, from concept to execution

Additionally, you will:

  • Deliver tailored interventions with external partners
  • Contribute to monthly fellowship reports
  • Continuously improve program effectiveness

3. Networking and Ecosystem Building

A major part of your work will involve building connections across Africa’s entrepreneurial ecosystem:

  • Link entrepreneurs to industry experts, mentors, and investors
  • Identify strategic partners across different countries
  • Support expansion of the Anzisha network

4. Talent Identification and Selection

You will help discover the next generation of entrepreneurs by:

  • Identifying promising candidates
  • Supporting the selection process
  • Reviewing and grading applications and assignments

5. Tracking, Evaluation, and Reporting

Data-driven decision-making is central to this role. You will:

  • Monitor entrepreneur performance
  • Track progress against KPIs
  • Provide detailed performance reports
  • Use insights to improve program delivery

6. Collaboration and Thought Leadership

As a visible representative of the program, you will:

  • Share insights through articles, podcasts, and events
  • Participate in outreach activities and information sessions
  • Support content creation and storytelling
  • Conduct research on challenges facing African entrepreneurs

Minimum Requirements and Qualifications

Educational Background

  • Bachelor’s degree in:
    • Business
    • Entrepreneurship
    • Or a related field
  • A Master’s degree is an added advantage

Professional Experience

Candidates must have:

  • Proven experience as:
    • Entrepreneur
    • Business consultant
    • Coach
  • Strong understanding of entrepreneurship principles
  • Experience working with young people
  • Demonstrated leadership and emotional intelligence

Core Skills Required

  • Project management and execution
  • Data analysis and KPI tracking
  • Problem-solving and critical thinking
  • Communication and interpersonal skills
  • Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities

Preferred Qualifications

  • Financial expertise and investor network
  • Experience running a business in Africa
  • Multilingual ability (especially French)
  • Strong stakeholder management skills

Competencies and Personal Attributes

To succeed in this role, candidates must demonstrate:

Technical Competencies

  • Coaching and mentorship
  • Program development
  • Networking and partnerships
  • Monitoring and evaluation

Personal Attributes

  • High integrity and professionalism
  • Emotional maturity and resilience
  • Strong interpersonal and conflict-resolution skills
  • Ability to work under pressure
  • Initiative and proactive mindset

Who Can Apply?

Eligible Candidates

This opportunity is open to:

  • Professionals with mid to senior-level experience
  • Individuals passionate about entrepreneurship and youth development
  • Candidates with experience working in or with African markets

Geographic Eligibility

  • Open to applicants across Africa
  • Remote role, but:
    • Preference is given to candidates based in South Africa
    • Must be willing to travel across African countries

Apply here

Why This Opportunity Matters

This is more than a job—it is a chance to:

  • Shape Africa’s entrepreneurial future
  • Mentor the next generation of business leaders
  • Work within a high-impact, pan-African organization
  • Contribute to job creation and economic development

By joining the African Leadership Academy, you become part of a mission-driven ecosystem committed to transforming Africa through leadership and innovation.

Deadline

17th April 2026

The Anzisha Coach role is ideal for individuals who combine entrepreneurial experience, coaching ability, and a passion for impact. It offers a rare opportunity to influence both individual lives and broader economic systems across Africa.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Anzisha Coach Job Opportunity 2026 (Remote) at African Leadership Academy: Empower Africa’s Next Generation of Entrepreneurs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Launch Your Global Career: Apply for NATO Internships 2026 in the Netherlands
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
Looking to gain international experience with a globally recognized organization? The NATO Internships 2026 at Joint Force Command Brunssum (JFCBS) offer students a unique opportunity to work across diverse fields such as political affairs, cybersecurity, logistics, and legal services. Based in the Netherlands, this paid internship program provides hands-on experience, professional exposure, and the chance […]

The post Launch Your Global Career: Apply for NATO Internships 2026 in the Netherlands appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Looking to gain international experience with a globally recognized organization? The NATO Internships 2026 at Joint Force Command Brunssum (JFCBS) offer students a unique opportunity to work across diverse fields such as political affairs, cybersecurity, logistics, and legal services. Based in the Netherlands, this paid internship program provides hands-on experience, professional exposure, and the chance to contribute to real-world international operations. Applications close on 22 April 2026.

About the Opportunity

The North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO) is offering multiple internship opportunities at its Joint Force Command Brunssum (JFCBS) headquarters in the Netherlands. This program is designed to give students practical experience in an international, multidisciplinary environment.

Interns will have the opportunity to work across a wide range of departments, depending on their interests and academic background. Available internship areas include:

  • Psychological Operations (PsyOps)
  • Political Affairs (POLAD)
  • Legal Affairs (LEGAD)
  • Civil-Military Cooperation (CIMIC)
  • Budget and Finance (J8 Budget)
  • Education and Training (J7 E&IT)
  • Cyberspace and IT (J6 CYD)
  • Logistics and Information Systems (J4 LISS)
  • Provost Marshal Office (PMO)
  • Integrated Advisory Team (IAT)

These internships allow participants to contribute to ongoing projects, support operational functions, and gain insight into how NATO operates across multiple domains.

The internships are based in Brunssum, Netherlands, and are structured as part of NATO’s broader effort to engage and train the next generation of professionals in international security, policy, and operations.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Internships at NATO provide more than just work experience—they offer insight into how international cooperation functions in practice. As global challenges become increasingly complex, there is a growing need for professionals who understand multidisciplinary approaches to security, governance, and collaboration.

By working across areas such as cybersecurity, logistics, political affairs, and civil-military cooperation, interns gain a holistic understanding of how large international organizations operate. This exposure is especially valuable for students aiming to pursue careers in global institutions, government, or international development.

Additionally, the opportunity to work in a multicultural environment helps participants develop essential skills such as communication, adaptability, and cross-cultural collaboration. These are critical competencies in today’s interconnected world.

Overall, the NATO internship program equips students with both technical knowledge and practical experience, preparing them for future roles in international careers.

What You’ll Gain

This internship provides a range of valuable professional and financial benefits:

  • Monthly stipend of €1,477.30, providing financial support during the internship
  • Reimbursement of travel costs (within set limits)
  • Hands-on experience in an international and highly professional environment
  • Exposure to global operations, policy-making, and strategic processes
  • Opportunity to work across multiple disciplines, from cybersecurity to political affairs
  • Networking opportunities with international professionals and experts
  • Structured work schedule, including 2 days of leave per month

This opportunity is ideal for students seeking to build a career in international relations, security, policy, or technical fields.

Eligibility

To be eligible for the NATO internship program, applicants must:

  • Be currently enrolled in full-time education
  • Ensure that the internship is part of their academic program
  • Remain enrolled for the entire duration of the internship

Applicants must also meet the application requirements, including submitting all necessary documents through the official platform.

It is important to note that applications prepared using generative AI tools will not be accepted, so candidates must ensure that all submitted materials are their own original work.

Application Process and Deadline

To apply for the NATO internship program, candidates must follow these steps carefully:

  1. Apply through the NATO Talent Acquisition Platform (NTAP):
    Complete the application form via the official platform.
  2. Select an Internship Opportunity:
    Choose the specific internship at HQ JFC Brunssum that aligns with your interests (e.g., PsyOps, Legal, Cyberspace, Logistics, etc.).
  3. Submit Required Documents:
    • CV/Resume
    • One-page motivation letter explaining your interest, expectations, and objectives
  4. Ensure Completeness:
    Incomplete applications will not be considered.

Deadline: 22 April 2026 (11:59 PM)

To learn more and apply, visit the official NATO internship portal.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth
Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities

The post Launch Your Global Career: Apply for NATO Internships 2026 in the Netherlands appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Unlock a PR Career Boost: Apply for the Ketchum PRISM Fellowship 2026
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
If you’re a graduate student looking to break into the public relations industry, the Ketchum PRISM Fellowship 2026 offers a powerful opportunity to gain hands-on experience, earn competitive pay, and publish your research. This prestigious summer fellowship combines real-world PR work with research and industry exposure, making it ideal for students ready to take their […]

The post Unlock a PR Career Boost: Apply for the Ketchum PRISM Fellowship 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you’re a graduate student looking to break into the public relations industry, the Ketchum PRISM Fellowship 2026 offers a powerful opportunity to gain hands-on experience, earn competitive pay, and publish your research. This prestigious summer fellowship combines real-world PR work with research and industry exposure, making it ideal for students ready to take their careers to the next level. Applications close on April 17, 2026.

About the Opportunity

The Ketchum PRISM Fellowship for Excellence in Public Relations Research, Insights, Strategy, and Measurement is a highly competitive program designed for graduate students in public relations, communications, business, and related fields.

Organized by Ketchum Analytics in collaboration with the Institute for Public Relations (IPR), this fellowship focuses on bridging the gap between academic research and real-world PR practice. It emphasizes “research that matters,” equipping participants with the ability to generate insights that directly impact communication strategies and business decisions.

The fellowship runs for eight weeks during the summer (mid-June to mid-August 2026) and takes place in person at one of Ketchum’s offices in New York City, Chicago, or Dallas. During this period, fellows will work closely with Ketchum’s Analytics and Strategy teams, contributing to real client projects and gaining exposure to the inner workings of a global PR agency.

In addition to practical experience, fellows are required to produce a research paper aimed at industry practitioners. This paper, which should incorporate modern tools and innovative approaches, may be published on the Institute for Public Relations website—offering significant professional visibility.

What You’ll Gain

This fellowship offers a combination of financial, professional, and academic benefits:

  • Paid experience at $22 per hour for approximately 40 hours per week
  • Hands-on PR industry exposure, working on real projects with experienced professionals
  • Research publication opportunity on the Institute for Public Relations platform
  • $2,500 grant awarded upon acceptance of the research paper
  • Industry recognition, including participation in the IPR Annual Awards Gala in New York City
  • Skill development in analytics, strategy, and communication research
  • Networking opportunities with leading PR professionals and researchers

This opportunity is particularly valuable for students aiming to build a career in public relations, communications strategy, or research and analytics.

Why This Opportunity Matters

In today’s data-driven communication landscape, public relations is no longer just about messaging—it is about insights, measurement, and strategic decision-making. Organizations increasingly rely on research to understand audiences, evaluate campaigns, and drive impact.

The Ketchum PRISM Fellowship addresses this shift by training the next generation of PR professionals to think critically, analyze data, and produce meaningful insights. By combining hands-on agency experience with research development, the program ensures that participants are well-prepared for the evolving demands of the industry.

Moreover, the opportunity to publish research and present findings to industry audiences gives fellows a competitive edge. It allows them to build credibility, expand their professional network, and position themselves as thought leaders early in their careers.

For students seeking to stand out in a competitive job market, this fellowship offers both practical experience and long-term career value.

Eligibility

To be eligible for the Ketchum PRISM Fellowship, applicants must:

  • Be a graduate student (Master’s or PhD) in public relations, communications, business, or a related research field
  • Have completed at least one year of study
  • Demonstrate fluency in written and spoken English
  • Be able to work at least three days per week in person at a Ketchum office (New York City, Chicago, or Dallas)
  • Be available to work approximately 40 hours per week during the fellowship period

Post-doctoral candidates are also encouraged to apply.

Applicants should have a strong interest in public relations research, measurement, and strategy, as well as the ability to translate complex ideas into practical insights.

Application Process and Deadline

To apply for the Ketchum PRISM Fellowship, candidates must prepare and submit the following materials:

  • Statement of Interest: Explain why you are a strong candidate for the fellowship
  • Research Prospectus (max 1,000 words): Outline the research topic you plan to explore
  • Resume: Highlight relevant academic, research, or professional experience

Applications are reviewed by a panel of public relations researchers and practitioners. Shortlisted candidates will be invited for interviews with Ketchum.

Deadline: April 17, 2026
Fellowship Period: Mid-June to mid-August 2026
Final Paper Deadline: November 10, 2026

To learn more and apply, visit the official Ketchum PRISM website.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth
Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Unlock a PR Career Boost: Apply for the Ketchum PRISM Fellowship 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: McCain Global Leaders Ukraine Fellowship 2026
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
Are you a Ukrainian professional ready to shape your country’s future? The McCain Global Leaders Ukraine Fellowship is now open, offering a unique opportunity to develop leadership skills, engage with global experts, and contribute to Ukraine’s post-war reconstruction. This fully funded, three-month hybrid fellowship combines online learning with an in-person workshop in Bosnia and Herzegovina, providing […]

The post Apply Now: McCain Global Leaders Ukraine Fellowship 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you a Ukrainian professional ready to shape your country’s future? The McCain Global Leaders Ukraine Fellowship is now open, offering a unique opportunity to develop leadership skills, engage with global experts, and contribute to Ukraine’s post-war reconstruction. This fully funded, three-month hybrid fellowship combines online learning with an in-person workshop in Bosnia and Herzegovina, providing both practical skills and international exposure.

About the Opportunity

The McCain Global Leaders Ukraine Fellowship is a pilot initiative launched by the McCain Institute at Arizona State University. It is designed to support a new generation of Ukrainian leaders committed to rebuilding a democratic, inclusive, and resilient Ukraine.

The fellowship honors the legacy of Senator John McCain, who was a strong advocate for Ukraine’s sovereignty and democratic development. Through this program, the McCain Institute aims to equip Ukrainian professionals with the tools, knowledge, and networks needed to contribute meaningfully to post-war recovery efforts.

This three-month hybrid program will run from July to September 2026. Participants will engage in a combination of virtual leadership modules, thematic discussions, and collaborative work with peers from diverse sectors.

A key highlight of the fellowship is a fully funded, week-long in-person workshop in Sarajevo, Bosnia and Herzegovina (August 15–22, 2026). This immersive experience allows fellows to connect in person, deepen their learning, and collaborate on solutions for Ukraine’s reconstruction.

Throughout the program, fellows will work together to develop recommendations for a democracy-centered and inclusive reconstruction process, while also creating actionable plans to apply their learning within their own communities and sectors.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Ukraine’s reconstruction will require strong, visionary leadership across multiple sectors. Beyond infrastructure rebuilding, the country faces complex challenges related to governance, economic recovery, social cohesion, and environmental sustainability.

This fellowship addresses these challenges by investing in people—equipping leaders with the skills and networks necessary to drive meaningful change. By fostering collaboration across sectors, the program encourages holistic solutions that reflect the interconnected nature of reconstruction efforts.

Importantly, the fellowship emphasizes democracy, inclusion, and community-driven approaches, ensuring that rebuilding efforts are not only effective but also equitable. Participants will gain the tools to influence policy, mobilize resources, and implement solutions that benefit their communities.

In a global context, this initiative highlights the importance of international cooperation and knowledge exchange, bringing together local expertise and global perspectives to address one of the most pressing challenges of our time.

What You’ll Gain

This fellowship offers a powerful combination of professional development and global exposure:

  • Fully funded participation, including travel and accommodation for the international workshop
  • Advanced leadership training focused on real-world challenges in post-war reconstruction
  • Technical skills development in areas such as communication and proposal writing
  • Access to international experts and practitioners in reconstruction and policy
  • Networking opportunities within the Washington, D.C. policy space
  • Cross-sector collaboration with leaders from diverse backgrounds
  • Practical impact, through action plans and policy recommendations

This is an exceptional opportunity for Ukrainian professionals looking to expand their influence, strengthen their expertise, and contribute to national recovery efforts.

Eligibility

To apply for the McCain Global Leaders Ukraine Fellowship, candidates must meet the following criteria:

  • Be based in Ukraine
  • Have at least six years of relevant professional experience
  • Demonstrate working proficiency in English
  • Have permission from the Ukrainian government to travel internationally for the August workshop

The program is open to professionals from a wide range of sectors, including:

  • Policy
  • Veterans
  • Civil society
  • Business and technology
  • Environment and ecology
  • Media
  • Culture

The fellowship strongly encourages applications from individuals outside the Kyiv region, promoting geographic diversity and inclusion.

Application Process and Deadline

To apply for the fellowship, candidates must prepare and submit the following materials:

  • A CV (maximum 2 pages)
  • A cover letter (maximum 1 page) explaining your motivation and relevant experience
  • An essay (maximum 1 page) addressing:
    • Key post-war reconstruction challenges in your sector
    • How these challenges connect with other sectors
    • How the fellowship will help you create impact

Applications must be submitted through the official application platform. Candidates should ensure all materials are complete, as incomplete applications will not be considered.

Deadline: 30 April 2026 (17:00 EST)

Applicants must also be willing to undergo a background check as part of the selection process.

To learn more and apply, visit the official McCain Institute website.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth
Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Apply Now: McCain Global Leaders Ukraine Fellowship 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Constellation Fund Grant 2026: A Comprehensive Funding Opportunity to Scale Impact in Education, Employment, and Health
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
Organizations committed to tackling poverty and driving meaningful social change have a significant opportunity to secure funding through the Constellation Fund Grant Cycle for April 2026. This initiative is designed to support high-impact programs that improve the lives of individuals and families living below the federal poverty threshold, with a strong emphasis on scalability, sustainability, […]

The post Constellation Fund Grant 2026: A Comprehensive Funding Opportunity to Scale Impact in Education, Employment, and Health appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Organizations committed to tackling poverty and driving meaningful social change have a significant opportunity to secure funding through the Constellation Fund Grant Cycle for April 2026. This initiative is designed to support high-impact programs that improve the lives of individuals and families living below the federal poverty threshold, with a strong emphasis on scalability, sustainability, and measurable outcomes.

As economic inequality and access disparities continue to affect communities, especially in urban and underserved areas, targeted funding mechanisms like the Constellation Fund play a critical role in empowering organizations that are already delivering solutions on the ground.

A Strategic Approach to Fighting Poverty

The Constellation Fund distinguishes itself by focusing on solutions that are closest to the challenges they aim to solve. Rather than supporting theoretical or early-stage ideas alone, the fund prioritizes organizations and programs that have demonstrated effectiveness and are ready to expand their reach.

This strategic approach ensures that funding is directed toward initiatives that not only address immediate needs but also create long-term, systemic change. By investing in proven interventions, the fund helps amplify impact and accelerate progress toward poverty reduction.

Focus Areas for the 2026 Grant Cycle

For the April 2026 application round, the Constellation Fund is concentrating on three core sectors that are essential to improving quality of life and economic mobility:

Education

Education remains one of the most powerful tools for breaking the cycle of poverty. The fund supports programs that enhance access to quality education, improve learning outcomes, and equip individuals with skills necessary for future employment. This includes initiatives focused on youth development, adult education, and workforce training.

Employment

Economic stability is directly linked to employment opportunities. The fund seeks to support programs that help individuals secure stable jobs, increase their income, and build sustainable livelihoods. This may include job training programs, career readiness initiatives, and partnerships with employers.

Health

Health and well-being are fundamental to personal and community resilience. The fund supports organizations that provide access to healthcare services, promote mental health, and address social determinants of health. Programs that integrate health with economic and educational outcomes are particularly valuable.

These focus areas are interconnected, reflecting the understanding that poverty is a multidimensional issue requiring integrated solutions.

Who Can Apply and What Makes a Strong Candidate

To be eligible for this funding opportunity, organizations must operate within specific counties in Minnesota, including Anoka, Carver, Dakota, Hennepin, Ramsey, Scott, and Washington, all within the broader metropolitan area of Minneapolis.

In addition, applicants must:

  • Primarily serve individuals living below 200% of the federal poverty guidelines
  • Deliver programs aligned with one or more of the fund’s core impact areas
  • Demonstrate a track record of measurable impact
  • Show strong potential for growth or scalability

Organizations that are most competitive are those that can clearly articulate their impact, provide data or evidence of success, and present a compelling vision for how additional funding will enable them to expand or deepen their work.

Application Timeline and Process

The application window for the Constellation Fund Grant 2026 is limited, making early preparation essential:

  • Applications Open: 1 April 2026
  • Deadline: 30 April 2026

All applications submitted during this period will be reviewed for consideration in the Fall 2026 grant round.

The application process is designed to be accessible while still rigorous. Applicants are encouraged to review available resources, including a video overview and frequently asked questions, to better understand expectations and requirements.

Organizations may also reach out directly to program officers for clarification or guidance, ensuring that applicants can submit the strongest possible proposals.

What the Fund Looks For

The Constellation Fund is particularly interested in:

  • Programs with a proven model that can be replicated or scaled
  • Organizations that demonstrate strong leadership and operational capacity
  • Initiatives that address root causes of poverty rather than only symptoms
  • Clear plans for measuring outcomes and tracking progress

This focus ensures that funding is not only impactful in the short term but also contributes to sustainable development and long-term community resilience.

Beyond Funding: Building Long-Term Impact

Receiving a grant from the Constellation Fund is not just about financial support—it is also about becoming part of a broader ecosystem of changemakers. Funded organizations gain recognition and validation that can help attract additional partners, donors, and opportunities.

Moreover, the emphasis on scalability and impact encourages organizations to think strategically about growth, innovation, and long-term sustainability.

Final Thoughts

The Constellation Fund Grant 2026 represents a powerful opportunity for organizations working at the forefront of poverty reduction to scale their impact and reach more communities in need. By focusing on education, employment, and health, and by supporting proven, high-impact initiatives, the fund plays a crucial role in advancing equitable and sustainable development.

For organizations ready to take their work to the next level, this grant offers not only financial support but also a platform to amplify their mission and create lasting change.

For more information click here 

APPLY HERE 

Discover more information here 

The post Constellation Fund Grant 2026: A Comprehensive Funding Opportunity to Scale Impact in Education, Employment, and Health appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Project & Development Associate Job 2026 in Vienna: Work with the Ban Ki-moon Foundation on Global Impact Initiatives
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Ban Ki-moon Foundation is offering an exciting career opportunity for early-career professionals passionate about global development, sustainability, and international policy. The Project & Development Associate position, based in Vienna, provides a unique gateway into the world of international organizations and high-level global engagement. This full-time role is designed for motivated individuals eager to contribute […]

The post Project & Development Associate Job 2026 in Vienna: Work with the Ban Ki-moon Foundation on Global Impact Initiatives appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Ban Ki-moon Foundation is offering an exciting career opportunity for early-career professionals passionate about global development, sustainability, and international policy. The Project & Development Associate position, based in Vienna, provides a unique gateway into the world of international organizations and high-level global engagement.

This full-time role is designed for motivated individuals eager to contribute to initiatives aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and the Paris Climate Agreement while gaining hands-on experience in project management, partnerships, and fundraising.

About the Ban Ki-moon Foundation

The Ban Ki-moon Foundation is a quasi-international organization working to empower current and future leaders to advance sustainable development worldwide. With strong connections to global institutions, including the United Nations, the Foundation plays a vital role in promoting climate action, education, gender equality, and youth engagement.

Position Overview

The Project & Development Associate will support senior leadership and contribute to a wide range of strategic and operational activities. The role combines project coordination, fundraising, stakeholder engagement, and administrative support, making it ideal for candidates seeking a dynamic and multifaceted work environment.

The contract is initially offered for 12 months, with the possibility of extension based on performance and organizational needs.

Key Responsibilities

Fundraising and Partnerships

  • Support the development and implementation of fundraising strategies
  • Assist in drafting proposals for international donors, including governments, EU grants, and private sector partners
  • Conduct research to identify funding opportunities and maintain internal databases
  • Coordinate grant proposal processes and support technical documentation

Project Management

  • Assist in implementing foundation projects such as scholarships and mentorship programs
  • Support the organization of events, including logistics, coordination, and reporting
  • Monitor project deliverables and evaluate impact
  • Contribute to communication materials, including briefs and social media content

Strategic and Administrative Support

  • Provide assistance to senior management, including calendar coordination and meeting preparation
  • Conduct background research for high-level engagements
  • Support collaboration with international partners and stakeholders

Skills and Qualifications

Required:

  • 1–2 years of experience in proposal writing and/or project management
  • Excellent English language skills
  • Strong written and verbal communication abilities
  • Interest in sustainability, gender equality, education, and environmental issues
  • Ability to work collaboratively and independently

Desirable:

  • Experience in NGOs or international organizations
  • Knowledge of fundraising and partnership development
  • Additional language skills (German and/or French)
  • Familiarity with digital tools such as MS Office, Zoom, and Canva

Salary and Benefits

  • Monthly net salary starting at 2,000 EUR, paid 13 times per year
  • Approximate annual gross salary of 34,913 EUR
  • Salary exempt from Austrian income tax (subject to social security contributions)
  • Five weeks of paid vacation and public holidays
  • Flexible work arrangements, including up to 100 days of remote work per year

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must have the legal right to work in Austria for the entire duration of the contract. Non-EU candidates must provide a valid work permit at the time of application.

Application Process

To apply, candidates must submit the following documents in English:

  • Curriculum Vitae
  • Cover Letter
  • A 2-minute video introducing themselves, their experience, and motivation

Applications should be sent via email to the Foundation’s recruitment address.

Important Dates

  • Deadline for applications: 20 April 2026
  • Start date: As soon as possible

Why Apply?

This position offers a rare opportunity to work closely with global leaders and contribute to impactful international initiatives. It is particularly suited for candidates aiming to build careers in international organizations, NGOs, or global policy institutions.

For more information click here 

Discover more opportunities here

The post Project & Development Associate Job 2026 in Vienna: Work with the Ban Ki-moon Foundation on Global Impact Initiatives appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: Remote Climate Researcher/Writer Role with the Institute for Human Rights and Business
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
Are you an experienced researcher or writer passionate about climate adaptation and social impact? The Institute for Human Rights and Business (IHRB) is hiring 1–2 Researchers/Writers to lead global storytelling and research on climate resilience. This fully remote consultancy offers a competitive package of approximately £70,000 over 18 months, along with the opportunity to influence […]

The post Apply Now: Remote Climate Researcher/Writer Role with the Institute for Human Rights and Business appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you an experienced researcher or writer passionate about climate adaptation and social impact? The Institute for Human Rights and Business (IHRB) is hiring 1–2 Researchers/Writers to lead global storytelling and research on climate resilience. This fully remote consultancy offers a competitive package of approximately £70,000 over 18 months, along with the opportunity to influence climate policy and amplify community-led solutions worldwide. Applications close on 15 April 2026.

About the Opportunity

The Institute for Human Rights and Business (IHRB) is an international think tank founded in 2009, working at the intersection of business, human rights, and sustainability. Through collaboration with governments, companies, and civil society, IHRB aims to promote accountability and ensure that human rights are embedded in global business practices.

This opportunity is part of IHRB’s JUST Stories project, a flagship initiative focused on documenting and sharing real-world examples of community-led climate adaptation and resilience. The project highlights how local communities—especially those on the frontlines of climate change—are designing and implementing solutions that can inform global policy and investment decisions.

The selected candidates will play a leading role in the second phase of the JUST Stories project, which focuses on producing high-quality, evidence-based narratives. These stories are designed to influence decision-makers across sectors such as government, finance, and civil society, while authentically representing the voices and experiences of communities.

This role combines research, storytelling, and policy engagement, making it ideal for professionals who can bridge academic analysis with compelling narrative writing.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Climate adaptation is one of the most urgent global priorities, particularly for communities that are already experiencing the impacts of climate change. However, many existing solutions fail to fully incorporate the perspectives and knowledge of those most affected.

The JUST Stories project addresses this gap by documenting and elevating community-led solutions, ensuring that these approaches inform global strategies and decision-making processes. By focusing on real-world examples, the project helps bridge the gap between policy and practice.

This role is especially important because it combines evidence-based research with powerful storytelling, enabling complex ideas to reach and influence a broader audience. The selected candidates will contribute to shaping more inclusive, effective, and human-centered climate policies.

What You’ll Gain

This role offers a unique combination of professional and global impact benefits:

  • Competitive Compensation: Earn approximately £70,000 over the contract period.
  • Global Influence: Contribute to research that informs climate adaptation policies and investment decisions.
  • Meaningful Work: Amplify the voices of frontline communities and showcase real-world solutions.
  • International Exposure: Engage with global stakeholders and participate in high-level events.
  • Flexible Work: Work remotely with a flexible schedule while contributing to a major global initiative.

This opportunity is particularly valuable for professionals seeking to combine research, storytelling, and advocacy in the climate and development space.

Eligibility

Applicants should meet the following requirements:

  • At least 7 years of experience in research, writing, or related fields
  • Strong expertise in climate adaptation and resilience, particularly community-led approaches
  • Proven experience in qualitative research, interviews, and long-form writing
  • Familiarity with frameworks such as UN climate adaptation or resilience frameworks
  • Excellent analytical and storytelling skills

Additional desirable qualifications include:

  • Experience working with Indigenous communities or vulnerable populations
  • Background in journalism, development studies, anthropology, or human rights
  • Knowledge of climate finance, policy, or philanthropy sectors
  • Additional language skills relevant to project regions

Candidates must also be able to work remotely, manage their own schedule, and collaborate effectively across time zones.

Application Process and Deadline

To apply for the Researcher/Writer role:

  1. Complete the Application Form: Fill out the official online application form.
  2. Prepare Supporting Information: Ensure your experience and expertise are clearly outlined.
  3. Submit Before Deadline: Applications must be submitted on time to be considered.

Deadline: 15 April 2026 (11:59 PM UK time)
Contract Period: April/May 2026 – September 2027
Location: Remote (Global)

Shortlisted candidates will be invited for interviews scheduled for 23–24 April 2026.

To learn more and apply, visit the job posting here.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth
Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Apply Now: Remote Climate Researcher/Writer Role with the Institute for Human Rights and Business appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Free Online Course 2026: Master Entrepreneurship in the Sport Sector with ITC and WIPO
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
In celebration of the International Day of Sport for Development and Peace, a new and impactful learning opportunity has been launched for aspiring entrepreneurs and innovators worldwide. The Entrepreneurship in Sport Sector E-learning Course, developed jointly by the International Trade Centre and the World Intellectual Property Organization, offers participants the chance to transform their passion […]

The post Free Online Course 2026: Master Entrepreneurship in the Sport Sector with ITC and WIPO appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
In celebration of the International Day of Sport for Development and Peace, a new and impactful learning opportunity has been launched for aspiring entrepreneurs and innovators worldwide. The Entrepreneurship in Sport Sector E-learning Course, developed jointly by the International Trade Centre and the World Intellectual Property Organization, offers participants the chance to transform their passion for sport into sustainable and profitable ventures.

This free, self-paced online course is designed to equip learners with essential entrepreneurial and intellectual property (IP) skills tailored specifically to the sports industry.

Why This Course Matters

Sport is more than competition and entertainment—it is a powerful engine for social inclusion, economic growth, and community resilience. Across the globe, the sports sector is creating opportunities for innovation, employment, and sustainable development.

This course highlights how sport can be leveraged as a platform for entrepreneurship while emphasizing the importance of protecting ideas, brands, and innovations through intellectual property tools.

What You Will Learn

Participants will gain comprehensive knowledge in two key areas:

Entrepreneurship in the Sports Sector

  • Identifying market opportunities within the sports industry
  • Building strong brands and sponsorship strategies
  • Exploring business models for micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs)
  • Understanding the broader sports value chain

Intellectual Property in Sport

  • Using IP tools to protect business ideas and innovations
  • Managing trademarks, designs, and branding assets
  • Commercializing intellectual property effectively
  • Enhancing competitiveness through IP strategies

Flexible Learning Experience

The course is fully online and self-paced, allowing participants to learn at their convenience. This flexibility makes it accessible to learners from different backgrounds, including students, professionals, and entrepreneurs.

Course Outcomes

By the end of the program, participants will be able to:

  • Develop viable business ideas within the sports sector
  • Apply intellectual property strategies to protect and grow their ventures
  • Create sustainable, inclusive, and market-ready business models
  • Contribute to economic development through sport-driven entrepreneurship

Who Should Enroll

This course is ideal for:

  • Aspiring and established entrepreneurs
  • Sports federations and clubs
  • Creative and tech startups
  • Organizations supporting business development
  • Anyone interested in entering or expanding within the sports industry

Enrollment Periods and Deadlines

The course is offered in three different cohorts throughout 2026:

  • April 2026 Cohort
    Registration opens: 6 April 2026
    Course closes: 30 April 2026
  • June 2026 Cohort
    Registration opens: 1 June 2026
    Course closes: 30 June 2026
  • November 2026 Cohort
    Registration opens: 2 November 2026
    Course closes: 30 November 2026

Participants can choose the cohort that best fits their schedule.

How to Apply

Interested learners can enroll directly through the official ITC learning platform using the link provided. Early registration is recommended to secure a spot in the preferred cohort.

A Strong Global Partnership

This course reflects the collaboration between the International Trade Centre and the World Intellectual Property Organization, two leading global institutions committed to empowering small businesses and fostering innovation through trade and intellectual property.

Final Thoughts

The Entrepreneurship in Sport Sector course is a valuable opportunity for anyone looking to merge business with passion. By combining entrepreneurship with intellectual property knowledge, participants are equipped to build resilient, innovative, and impactful ventures in the global sports economy.

For more information click here 

APPLY HERE 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Free Online Course 2026: Master Entrepreneurship in the Sport Sector with ITC and WIPO appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Climate Resilience Internship 2026 in Washington, DC: Gain Hands-On Experience in Climate Policy and Community Resilience
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Climate Resilience Internship at the Center for Climate and Energy Solutions offers a unique opportunity for students and early-career professionals to contribute to impactful climate initiatives while gaining real-world experience in policy research, stakeholder engagement, and sustainability strategy. Based in Washington, DC, this Summer 2026 internship provides a dynamic environment where interns work alongside […]

The post Climate Resilience Internship 2026 in Washington, DC: Gain Hands-On Experience in Climate Policy and Community Resilience appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Climate Resilience Internship at the Center for Climate and Energy Solutions offers a unique opportunity for students and early-career professionals to contribute to impactful climate initiatives while gaining real-world experience in policy research, stakeholder engagement, and sustainability strategy.

Based in Washington, DC, this Summer 2026 internship provides a dynamic environment where interns work alongside leading experts to address some of the most pressing challenges related to climate change and community resilience.

About C2ES

The Center for Climate and Energy Solutions (C2ES) is a highly respected, independent, and nonpartisan nonprofit organization dedicated to accelerating the transition to a low-carbon, resilient, and equitable economy. The organization collaborates with policymakers, businesses, and international stakeholders to drive meaningful climate action.

With a strong commitment to inclusivity, collaboration, and innovation, C2ES fosters a work culture that values diverse perspectives and emphasizes real-world impact.

Internship Overview

As a Climate Resilience Intern, you will work directly with the Climate Resilience Manager and program team to support research and engagement initiatives focused on strengthening community resilience to climate change.

The internship is full-time (up to 37.5 hours per week) during Summer 2026 and offers flexibility, allowing participants to work either in-person at the Washington, DC office or remotely from eligible U.S. states.

Key Responsibilities

Interns will engage in a variety of meaningful tasks, including:

  • Conducting independent research on climate resilience topics such as extreme heat policies, environmental regulations, and disaster preparedness
  • Supporting research on local and federal climate policies and resilience strategies
  • Assisting with stakeholder engagement and outreach for the Climate Resilient Communities Accelerator
  • Contributing to event planning, logistics, and coordination
  • Developing written outputs such as blog posts, policy briefs, and research summaries
  • Participating in interdisciplinary discussions and supporting broader program activities

Learning Opportunities

This internship provides exposure to cutting-edge climate resilience initiatives, particularly through involvement in the South-Central Puget Sound Accelerator. Interns will explore:

  • Nature-based solutions and urban planning strategies
  • Public-private partnerships in climate action
  • Economic development approaches linked to resilience
  • Data-driven and policy-oriented climate solutions

The program emphasizes both independent thinking and collaborative learning, allowing interns to tailor their research focus based on their academic background and interests.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • A strong interest in climate change, sustainability, or environmental policy
  • Excellent writing and analytical skills
  • The ability to work independently and collaboratively
  • A willingness to engage with diverse stakeholders
  • Academic background in fields such as environmental studies, engineering, political science, public policy, economics, or related disciplines

Internship Format and Benefits

  • Full-time internship (Summer 2026)
  • Hourly compensation of $20 per hour
  • Transportation stipend for in-person interns
  • Opportunity to work remotely from eligible U.S. states
  • Hands-on experience with a leading climate policy organization

Application Process

Applicants must submit the following:

  • Resume
  • Cover letter (maximum one page)
  • Academic transcripts
  • Writing sample (maximum five pages, preferably related to climate or environmental topics)

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so early submission is strongly encouraged.

Diversity and Inclusion Commitment

C2ES is deeply committed to fostering diversity, equity, inclusion, and justice within its organization and partnerships. The internship encourages applications from individuals of all backgrounds, ensuring a wide range of perspectives in advancing climate solutions.

Final Thoughts

The Climate Resilience Internship at C2ES is an excellent opportunity for aspiring climate professionals to gain practical experience, contribute to meaningful projects, and build a strong foundation in climate policy and resilience strategies. Whether working remotely or in Washington, DC, interns will be part of a mission-driven team shaping the future of climate action.

For more information click here 

APPLY HERE 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Climate Resilience Internship 2026 in Washington, DC: Gain Hands-On Experience in Climate Policy and Community Resilience appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
